title: lepisorus sinuatus
intro: Lepisorus sinuatus, also known as the Japanese lace fern, is a type of perennial fern plant native to East Asia, particularly in Japan and China. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Lepisorus sinuatus is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting skin health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea, tincture, or dried form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
